steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers hypothesized that each ecotype is locally adapted in ways that boost growth and resist pollutants. The results showed all ecotypes grew equally in all four water samples, and zinc boosted growth regardless of concentration. The blank must complete the logical conclusion those results suggest.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — if each ecotype is indeed locally adapted as the researchers hypothesized, those adaptations are to other environmental conditions than the water each ecotype inhabits.: the results rule out water composition and zinc as the basis for local adaptation, but the researchers didn't test light or temperature. This keeps the hypothesis viable while pointing to untested variables. ✓
  • Choice B — while the ecotypes are genetically and geographically distinct, those differences do not represent adaptations to local environmental conditions.: goes further than the data support — the experiment only tested water and zinc, so it can't rule out adaptation to other conditions (like light or temperature). ✗
  • Choice C — there may not be significant differences in the water that each ecotype inhabits, but there are significant differences in each ecotype's resistance to zinc pollution.: the results showed the opposite — zinc response was the same across all ecotypes. ✗
  • Choice D — although the researchers' hypothesis does not appear to be supported, this may be because the levels of zinc exposure the plants in the experiment received did not match their exposure in their natural environments.: focuses only on zinc levels as a confound, ignoring the equally notable water-sample result and the explicitly mentioned untested variables (light, temperature). ✗
That is the correct answer.
